title: Browser-Based Finerenone Meta-Analysis Validated Against R metafor
author: Mahmood Ahmad
body: Can a standalone browser application reproduce R metafor results for finerenone cardiovascular and renal trials across multiple effect measures? We implemented RapidMeta as a single 7,800-line HTML file executing DerSimonian-Laird and REML random-effects meta-analysis entirely client-side, covering odds ratios, risk ratios, and hazard ratios with HKSJ adjustment, Bayesian analysis, and Trial Sequential Analysis. All data were extracted from open-access sources including ClinicalTrials.gov, PubMed abstracts, and FDA documents for three Phase III trials enrolling 19,112 patients across FIDELIO-DKD, FIGARO-DKD, and FINEARTS-HF. Across 14 analyses spanning five outcomes, the application matched metafor to six decimal places for all estimates including MACE HR 0.87 (95% CI 0.79-0.95) and all-cause mortality HR 0.91 (95% CI 0.84-0.99). Concordance testing against 17 published meta-analyses showed 88 percent within 0.03 absolute difference. Browser-native JavaScript achieves reference-grade numerical accuracy for clinical meta-analysis without server infrastructure. The limitation of three-trial pooling means heterogeneity assessment has low power and results may shift as additional trials report.

sentences.text: Can a standalone browser application reproduce R metafor results for finerenone cardiovascular and renal trials across multiple effect measures?

We implemented RapidMeta as a single 7,800-line HTML file executing DerSimonian-Laird and REML random-effects meta-analysis entirely client-side, covering odds ratios, risk ratios, and hazard ratios with HKSJ adjustment, Bayesian analysis, and Trial Sequential Analysis.

All data were extracted from open-access sources including ClinicalTrials.gov, PubMed abstracts, and FDA documents for three Phase III trials enrolling 19,112 patients across FIDELIO-DKD, FIGARO-DKD, and FINEARTS-HF.

Across 14 analyses spanning five outcomes, the application matched metafor to six decimal places for all estimates including MACE HR 0.87 (95% CI 0.79-0.95) and all-cause mortality HR 0.91 (95% CI 0.84-0.99).
Concordance testing against 17 published meta-analyses showed 88 percent within 0.03 absolute difference.
Browser-native JavaScript achieves reference-grade numerical accuracy for clinical meta-analysis without server infrastructure.
The limitation of three-trial pooling means heterogeneity assessment has low power and results may shift as additional trials report.

notes.data: FIDELIO-DKD, FIGARO-DKD, FINEARTS-HF (N=19,112), OA sources only

checks.name: single paragraph
checks.detail: Body must not contain blank-line paragraph breaks.

checks.name: no headings
checks.detail: No markdown headings allowed in body.

checks.name: no links
checks.detail: No links or DOI links allowed in body.

checks.name: result sentence has interval
checks.detail: Sentence 4 should include a confidence interval.

checks.detail: Sentence 4 should name an effect measure or test metric.
